avoid: Replacing cuBLAS W@X with a custom full GEMM – high risk and unlikely to beat cuBLAS for large d
Hardcoding hidden dimensions or relying on shape-specific constants
Introducing fast-math approximations that could compromise deterministic correctness
evidence: All shapes show ~1% slowdown vs PyTorch baseline, aggregate speedup 0.9878
Custom rank-16 update kernel uses scalar accesses (vector_width=1), limiting memory throughput
Profile summary identifies bottleneck as rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth
d values (3584, 4096, 4608) are divisible by 4, enabling float4 vectorization
No correctness issues, so performance deficit is purely from kernel efficiency
recommended_actions: Increase vector_width to 4 (float4) for global memory loads/stores in the rank-16 update kernel
Ensure coalesced memory access patterns for A, B, X, and output Y
Experiment with block sizes (e.g., 64, 256) to balance occupancy and instruction-level parallelism
Consider using shared memory to cache B.T @ X intermediate (16 x d) to reduce redundant global reads
Unroll inner loops over rank dimension (16) to expose ILP and reduce loop overhead
